messages.key: amcis 2026 welcomes you to reno
messages.subject: AMCIS 2026 Welcomes you to Reno
messages.snippet: Greeting from the “Biggest Little City in the World,” Reno, Nevada where we will be hosting the America’s Conference on Information Systems August 20-22. As a premier global gathering for information systems academics and practitioners, the conference is dedicated to exploring how technology solves complex organizational and environmental challenges. This year, hundreds of scholars will converge in Reno to focus on our central theme: Digital Innovation. [...]

messages.snippet: Dear colleagues, We are pleased to invite submissions to the 18th Changing Nature of Work Workshop on Human-Digital Collaboration which will take place on Saturday, 12 December 2026 (11:00–17:00) in Lisbon. The workshop focuses on how digital technologies, including generative AI, virtual and augmented reality, algorithmic management, and new forms of digital work, are transforming work, organizing, and collaboration. We welcome contributions that deepen or challenge our understanding of the changing nature of work and the integration of human and digital factors in contemporary organizations. [...]

messages.key: episode 108 the invisible foundation why dns security is the governance gap no one is watching
messages.subject: Episode 108 -- The Invisible Foundation: Why DNS Security Is the Governance Gap No One Is Watching
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues: Episode 108 of the Cybersecurity Readiness Podcast is live, and it tackles a governance blind spot most boards have never once discussed: foundational network infrastructure — DNS, DHCP, and IP address management. My guest is Scott Harrell, President and CEO of Infoblox and former leader of Cisco's $20 billion enterprise networking business . We open with the October 2025 AWS outage — a single misconfiguration in a core routing service that cascaded into an estimated $581 million in losses. It wasn't a sophisticated attack. It was a governance decision nobody had made, because nobody was actively watching the layer [...]

messages.subject: FRecTour 2026 (co located with RecSys 2026) Call for Papers
messages.snippet: The tourism industry stands at the intersection of rapid technological advancements and evolving consumer behaviors, reshaping the way individuals explore, plan, and experience travel. The proliferation of information and communication technologies (ICT) and the widespread adoption of online platforms have ushered in a new era of travel, empowering travelers to curate bespoke experiences tailored to their unique preferences and interests. From researching destinations to booking accommodations and activities, today’s travelers rely heavily on digital companions and recommendation systems to navigate the vast landscape of tourism services and offerings. [...]

messages.key: digital sovereignty online paper development workshop for special issue in the information systems journal
messages.subject: Digital Sovereignty: Online Paper Development Workshop for Special Issue in the Information Systems Journal
messages.snippet: Call for Participation: Online Paper Development Workshop on Digital Sovereignty We are delighted to announce that we are arranging an online Paper Development Workshop (PDW) for our Information Systems Journal Special Issue on Digital Sovereignty. The aim of the PDW is to give prospective authors for the special issue the opportunity to meet the editors and present and discuss their preliminary work on the topic. To allow authors from different geographic regions of AIS to participate, the workshop will have three different sessions. Digital technologies, digital infrastructures, and platform ecosystems have become critical resources for nations, organisations, communities, and individuals. As a result, questions of who controls digital infrastructures, who governs data, and who sets the rules for digital innovation have become concerns for public administration, economic strategy, and everyday life. Artificial intelligence (AI) is perhaps the domain where the question of digital sovereignty is currently most acute — raising issues of dependence, available alternatives, and open-source solutions. Yet information systems research should have much to contribute, given its socio-technical approach to the study of digital technologies in context. There is a substantial body of information systems research relevant to the digital sovereignty discourse by addressing questions including how states and organisations can navigate global digital ecosystems, how digital architectures and governance standards can be designed to support digital sovereignty, how digital sovereignty influences digital innovation and the short- and long-term implications for individuals' and communities’ autonomy, rights, resilience and risks. In this special issue, we invite empirically grounded and theoretically ambitious research that explains digital sovereignty as a socio-technical accomplishment, including its tensions, trade-offs, and unintended consequences. We also welcome conceptual or review papers on the topic. We expect extended abstracts of studies based on qualitative research, but we also welcome ideas for quantitative contributions, provided they emphasise the research context and the theoretical and practical implications of their findings. Workshop details: Submission deadline: 6th September 2026 (11.59 pm UTC) Notification of acceptance: 18th September 2026 Updated information about the workshop:  https://www.mn.uio.no/ifi/english/research/groups/is/call-for-papers/online-paper-development-workshop-ds/index.html Session’s time and date: Asia-Pacific: September 24th 11:30 PM to 3:30 PM SGT Africa/Europe: September 24th 12:00 PM to 4 PM UTC Americas: September 28th 11:00 AM to 3 PM EDT Format: Online workshop with presentation and interaction with special issue editors Abstract format: Participation is based on an accepted extended abstract of up to 3 pages. The abstract must indicate a plan for a substantial information systems contribution foregrounding digital technologies in context, emphasising the specific nature of digital technologies, infrastructures, and platforms and their social implications Submission instructions Extended abstract of a maximum of 3 pages Email your submission to Coordinating Senior Editor: pnielsen@ifi.uio.no Indicate in the email the session of preference Indicate in the email who will present the work Special issue Submission deadline for full papers: March 31st 2027 Call for papers: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/page/journal/13652575/homepage/call-for-papers/si-2026-000456 Extended call for papers: https://www.mn.uio.no/ifi/english/research/groups/is/special-issue-on-digital-sovereignty.html Contact/questions: Petter Nielsen at pnielsen@ifi.uio.no For questions regarding this workshop, get in touch with Coordinating Senior Editor Petter Nielsen We are looking forward to seeing your contributions,  On behalf of the Senior Editors,  Petter Nielsen Daniel J. Veit Laura Brandimarte Juliana Sutanto Mary Tate Ben Eaton

ICSOC fosters cross-community scientific excellence by gathering experts from various disciplines, such as services science, data science, management science, business-process management, distributed systems, wireless and mobile computing, cloud and edge computing, cyber-physical systems, Internet of Things (IoT), scientific workflows, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and services [...]

messages.key: informs article the cryptographic reckoning why quantum readiness begins with agility not algorithms
messages.subject: INFORMS Article: The Cryptographic Reckoning Why Quantum Readiness Begins with Agility, Not Algorithms
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues: My new article, "The Cryptographic Reckoning: Why Quantum Readiness Begins with Agility, Not Algorithms," is live in INFORMS Analytics Magazine. -- https://pubsonline.informs.org/do/10.1287/LYTX.2026.02.06; -- https://www.dchatte.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/The-Cryptographic-Reckoning-_-Analytics-Magazine.pdf The core argument: post-quantum migration is failing in most enterprises — and the bottleneck is not algorithmic. It is organizational. The article applies the CPD Framework to diagnose exactly why — and what to do about it. The CPD Framework — first introduced in Cybersecurity Readiness (SAGE, 2021) — was built to surface these failure modes across contexts. Post-quantum migration is its latest proving ground. [...]

messages.snippet: Dear IS colleagues Many of us care deeply about making an impact through our research but recognize that academic incentives often get in the way. I'm writing to update you on a collective effort to address this problem, and to pay thanks to the AACSB and AOM for kicking it off. We will continue to update you, and please get involved as the initiative unfolds. Together we can change the system for the better. [...]

messages.snippet: CALL FOR PAPERS: SETN 2026 WORKSHOP KGCSW-SETN 2026: Knowledge Graph Construction Workshop OVERVIEW -------- The workshop on Knowledge Graph Construction is an essential step towards harnessing the transformative power of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in understanding complex data landscapes. Knowledge graphs stand out as pivotal tools for structuring data, making it interpretable and actionable by AI systems. They enable the representation of data in a form that mirrors human understanding, facilitating more natural interaction between humans and machines. [...]

messages.key: episode 107 compliant but exposed rethinking grc for real security
messages.subject: Episode 107 -- Compliant but Exposed: Rethinking GRC for Real Security
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues: Compliant. Certified. Breached. That's the uncomfortable reality facing too many organizations today — and it's exactly what Episode 107 of the Cybersecurity Readiness Podcast Series confronts head-on. My guest, Richa Kaul, Founder and CEO of Complyance<https://www.linkedin.com/in/richa-kaul/>, brings rare clarity to a problem that has plagued enterprise security governance for years: the dangerous conflation of compliance with security. When your GRC platform is full of green indicators, and your auditors just signed off — but your adversaries are already inside — something is fundamentally broken. [...]

messages.key: egovernment to combat corruption worldwide just published in jgitm june 22 2026
messages.subject: E-Government to Combat Corruption Worldwide just Published in JGITM, June 22, 2026
messages.snippet: E-Government to Combat Corruption Worldwide: Dominant Factors and a Holistic Model Emerging From Structured Literature Review Shailendra Palvia<https://www.tandfonline.com/author/Palvia%2C+Shailendra>, Sanjay Verma<https://www.tandfonline.com/author/Verma%2C+Sanjay>, Ambuj Anand<https://www.tandfonline.com/author/Anand%2C+Ambuj> This is a major study to comprehend the worldwide contribution of e-Government in combating corruption. One-third of the 164 articles reviewed were specific to one of the 17 countries: Argentina, Bangladesh, Brazil, China, Ethiopia, Fiji, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Jamaica, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Nepal, Nigeria, Romania, and South Africa. Almost 90 articles, in the global context, examined hypothesized models across 6 to 214 countries utilizing country-level indexes for several years. Utilizing Structured Literature Review (SLR) methodology, we conducted a [...]

messages.key: special issue the post ai enterprise deadline oct 15 2026 journal of it case and application research jitcar
messages.subject: Special Issue: The Post AI Enterprise | Deadline Oct 15, 2026 | Journal of IT Case and Application Research (JITCAR)
messages.snippet: Dear AIS Community, manuscripts are invited for the Journal of Information Technology Case and Application Research (JITCAR) Special Issue on The Post AI Enterprise. The Post AI Enterprise: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence signals a transition from systems that augment human work to those that increasingly participate in and shape knowledge creation itself. This special issue invites research on the emerging post-AI enterprise, where intelligence is deeply embedded across organizational and societal infrastructure and AI systems act not only as tools, but as autonomous or semi-autonomous contributors to discovery, decision-making, and coordination. Of particular interest is the rise of [...]

messages.key: historic milestone official launch of the ais gcc chapter
messages.subject: Historic Milestone: Official Launch of the AIS GCC Chapter
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues and Members of the Information Systems Community, It is my great pleasure to announce a historic milestone for the Information Systems (IS) discipline in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region. Following the successful GCC Information Systems Symposium, hosted by Carnegie Mellon University in Qatar on 20 November 2025, participants expressed overwhelming support for establishing a formal regional community for Information Systems scholars and practitioners. In our post-symposium survey, 100% of respondents endorsed the creation of an official GCC Chapter of the Association for Information Systems (AIS). [...]

messages.key: pacis 2026 workshop advancing ctc research in information systems jakarta indonesia
messages.subject: PACIS 2026 Workshop: Advancing CTC Research in Information Systems (Jakarta, Indonesia)
messages.snippet: Dear colleagues, If you are planning to attend PACIS 2026 in Jakarta, Indonesia, we warmly invite you to participate in our inaugural workshop on Advancing Computational Theory Construction (CTC) in Information Systems. This half-day workshop is designed to foster rich exchange and advance emerging research in CTC. The program includes: Keynote: Professor Suprateek Sarker (Editor-in-Chief, Information Systems Research ) will deliver a thought-provoking address on the future of qualitative research in the world of AI.  Panel: A distinguished panel featuring four Senior Editors from MIS Quarterly and Information Systems Research will share insights on publishing and shaping impactful research. Roundtables: Participants have the opportunity to engage in focused round-table discussions and receive feedback on their research ideas from leading faculty. Further details about the workshop can be found at the following link: https://pacis2026.aisconferences.org/events/workshops/advancing-computational-theory-construction-research-in-information-systems/ Although the abstract submission deadline has passed, please contact Shohil Kishore (s.kishore@auckland.ac.nz) if you would still like to submit an abstract.  We gratefully acknowledge the contributions of our mentors and organizers: Suprateek Sarker, Jungpil Hahn, Carol Hsu, Atreyi Kankanhalli, Chee-Wee Tan, Saeed Akhlaghpour, Chee Wei David Phang, Andrew Burton-Jones, Aron Lindberg, Saima Qutab, and Yvonne Hong, as well as the Department of Information Systems and Operations Management for their sponsorship. We look forward to your participation and to an engaging workshop. With best regards, CTC Team Shohil, Andrew, Aron, Yvonne, Saima

messages.key: call for papers for wise 2026
messages.subject: Call for Papers for WISE 2026
messages.snippet: We are pleased to announce that the 37th Workshop on Information Systems and Economics ( WISE 2026 ), will take place in Lisbon, Portugal, on December 16 - 18, 2026. We invite high-quality theoretical, empirical, experimental, and computational research on the economics of information systems, including AI, digital platforms, data and analytics, privacy and responsible technology, human–technology interaction, fintech, and related topics. The submission portal is now open at: www.paperfox.ai/conference/wise2026/submit Important Dates: -       Submission deadline: August 23, 2026, 11:59 PM Pacific Time. -       Decision notification: September 18, 2026. -       Early registration deadline: October 15, 2026. -       WISE 2026: December 16 - 18, 2026. For additional details about WISE 2026, the Call for Papers, and submission details, please visit: wiseconf.org . We look forward to receiving your submissions and seeing you in Lisbon. WISE 2026 Organizing Committee: Rodrigo Belo, Aaron Cheng, Pedro Ferreira, Miguel Godinho de Matos, Shachar Reichman, Daniel Rock, Maytal Saar-Tsechansky, and Vilma Todri.

messages.subject: [HICSS] CFP: The Metaverse for Work and Play Minitrack (with FAST-TRACK TO JIC)
messages.snippet: [cid:image002.png@01DCF1BA.563FB8E0] Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences (HICSS-60) Hilton Waikoloa Village, Big Island, Hawaii | January 5-8, 2027 Fast-track submission opportunity (at the discretion of minitrack chairs) in the Journal of Intellectual Capital: https://www.emeraldgrouppublishing.com/journal/jic Minitrack Description: Although the Metaverse presents significant opportunities, it also introduces a wide range of challenges that may hinder its successful adoption, including surveillance, user tracking, deviant behavior (e.g., bullying and stalking) (Lowry et al., 2017), design-related issues, unintended consequences such as addiction, technostress, anxiety, and cognitive overload (Xi et al., 2023), as well as emerging security and privacy threats (Vondrek et al., 2022). Lessons learned [...]

messages.key: call for papers hicss60 minitrack data science agentic ai and machine learning to support business decisions
messages.subject: Call for Papers — HICSS-60 Minitrack: Data Science, Agentic AI, and Machine Learning to Support Business Decisions
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues, As the co-chairs for the Data Science, Agentic AI, and Machine Learning to Support Business Decisions minitrack at the 60th Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences (HICSS-60), to be held January 5–8, 2027 at the Hilton Waikoloa Village on the Big Island of Hawaii, we would like to invite you to submit a paper and/or serve as a reviewer. Data science refers to the processing and analysis of data, in all its structured, unstructured, or multimodal varieties, to extract meaningful insights for business. Machine Learning and Generative AI have become so widespread that many business decisions are now [...]

messages.key: last call cfp hicss60 knowledge innovation and entrepreneurial systems track
messages.subject: Last Call CfP HICSS60 Knowledge, Innovation, and Entrepreneurial Systems Track
messages.snippet: Last Call! CALL FOR PAPERS Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences (HICSS-60), Hilton Waikoloa Village, Big Island, Hawaii, January 5-8, 2027 Track: Knowledge, Innovation, and Entrepreneurial Systems Knowledge Innovation and Entrepreneurial Systems focuses on the evolving nature of work and society. Competitive, political, and cultural pressures are forcing organizations to do more with less and to leverage all they know to succeed. Knowledge, innovation, and entrepreneurial systems are the systems we’re developing to facilitate collaboration, socialization, and work to improve knowledge capture, storage, transfer and flow. The use of knowledge and the systems that support it fosters creativity and innovation [...]

messages.key: last call cfp hicss60 ai in km minitrack
messages.subject: Last Call CfP HICSS60 AI in KM minitrack
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues, Last Call! I hope you are doing well. We are pleased to invite submissions to our minitrack titled: AI and Knowledge Management: Innovations, Challenges, Ethics, and Future Directions, at the upcoming Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences (HICSS)- 60. We welcome contributions that explore how AI is transforming knowledge creation, transfer, and use across domains. HICSS 60 marks an important milestone as the conference enters its sixth decade, continuing its legacy as one of the most influential and highly cited venues in Information Systems research. [...]

messages.key: launch of msc in government digital innovation and transformation
messages.subject: Launch of MSc in Government Digital Innovation and Transformation
messages.snippet: (Apologies for cross-posts) ONLINE M.SC. IN GOVERNMENT DIGITAL INNOVATION AND TRANSFORMATION (IGOV) (ENGLISH) The Department of Business Administration at the University of Thessaly offers a new Online Master of Science (M.Sc.) program in /GOVERNMENT DIGITAL INNOVATION AND TRANSFORMATION/. The program is offered fully in English and is certified by the Hellenic Authority for Higher Education. The Online M.Sc. in Government Digital Innovation and Transformation is a three-semester program (90 ECTS) that provides a global outlook that considers real-world trends while imparting relevant expertise for careers today in public administration. It focuses on the requirements and the processes of government [...]
